t_ch: 哈哈，看来你已经把善举当成了习惯。我认为我们有深入交流的必要。
t_en: Haha, it looks like you're a habitual altruist. I believe it necessary for us to have a deeper conversation.

t_ch: 我其实只想抖机灵…
t_en: I just wanted to pit my wits against him...

t_ch: 一个人的真实想法往往以玩笑的形式表露。我认为我们有深入交流的必要。
t_en: A person's true thoughts are often revealed in the form of jokes. I believe it necessary for us to have a deeper conversation.

t_ch: 这位小友，请不要急着离开。我还有些话要说。
t_en: Don't be in such a hurry to leave, young friend. I still have something to say.

t_ch: 所以，你到底是谁？
t_en: So, who exactly are you?

t_ch: 我名叫苏克提瑞斯，全奥赫玛最无知的老头，也是最虔诚的求知者。
t_en: My name is Soctires, the most witless geezer in Okhema, and a most devoted seeker of knowledge.

t_ch: 我听说过你，来自异邦的战士。圣城当下面临的问题，似乎也与你息息相关。
t_en: I have heard of you, warrior from the outlands. You also appear to be involved in the problems plaguing the holy city.

t_ch: 什么问题？
t_en: Yes?

t_ch: 如你所见，外邦人在这里正逐渐变得不受欢迎。
t_en: As you can see, outlanders are gradually becoming less welcome here.

t_ch: 你也是外邦人的一员。但你与黄金裔们并肩作战、证明了自己的勇气，这才被当作贵客——并非所有人都这么幸运。
t_en: You are also an outlander, but you have fought side by side with the Chrysos Heirs, proven your courage, and hailed as an esteemed guest. Not all are so lucky.

t_ch: 我也想过帮助那人来着…
t_en: I also thought about helping that person...

t_ch: 虽然没有付诸实践，但有行善的念头终究是好的——倘若其他公民都能学习你的品质，局势会比现在好上许多。
t_en: Even if not put into practice, the intent to do good is nonetheless commendable — if other citizens could learn from your virtue, we wouldn't be in such harrowing circumstances.

t_ch: 关我什么事？
t_en: What does that have to do with me?

t_ch: 或许你自恃受到尊敬，不会被凡人的纠纷困扰。但奥赫玛并没有表面上那么欢迎外邦人……
t_en: Perhaps because you are respected, therefore undisturbed by the woes of the common folk. But Okhema is not as welcoming of outlanders as it appears...

t_ch: 当这问题真正爆发时，民意势必如洪水般汹涌。我从未听说有人能因身份尊贵而免于淹溺。
t_en: When this problem truly explodes, public opinion is bound to surge forth like a deluge. A person's status has never saved them from drowning.

t_ch: 曾经的「永恒之城」并不吝于为其他城邦的来客敞开大门。但随着黑潮肆虐，人们心中的善意也逐渐萎靡……
t_en: This former Eternal City has always been generous in opening its doors to outsiders. But as the black tide encroaches, the goodwill in people's hearts may slowly give way to misgivings...

t_ch: 此事说来话长。我们应该找个能坐下的地方，舒舒服服地谈论这个议题——就选<color=#dbc291ff>云石天宫的浴场</color>吧，我是那里的尊享客户。
t_en: It is a long story. We should look for a place to speak about this more comfortably. How about <color=#dbc291ff>Marmoreal Palace's baths</color>? I am a privileged patron there.

t_ch: 不介意的话，我去开个浴池，你可以先在这周围逛逛。公民们还沉浸在刚才发生的事里，说不定能听到些有趣的看法。
t_en: If you don't mind, I'll go run a bath first. You can take a look around. The citizens are still mulling over what just happened, you might hear something of interest.

t_ch: 唉，这事儿最后怎么就皆大欢喜了呢？那我缺的乐子这块谁给补啊？
t_en: *sigh* Why did this matter have a happy ending? Who will fill up the hole of boredom within me now?

t_ch: 谁说不是呢？
t_en: Who said it isn't?

t_ch: 唉，果然你也被外邦人困扰过吧？有的外邦人确实不太守规矩……
t_en: *sigh* You have been troubled by outlanders too? Some of them really test the law's patience here...

t_ch: 你很讨厌外邦人吗？
t_en: You hate outlanders?

t_ch: 讨厌嘛，倒也算不上，就是有的外邦人确实不太守规矩……
t_en: I wouldn't say that, but some outlanders really flout the laws here...

t_ch: 有考虑过加入「酒馆」吗？
t_en: Have you considered joining a tavern?

t_ch: 那是什么，{RUBY_B#海洋之泰坦}法吉娜{RUBY_E#}信徒的聚集地？我可没兴趣……
t_en: What's that? A place where {RUBY_B#Ocean Titan}Phagousa{RUBY_E#}'s disciples congregate? I'm not interested...

t_ch: 我只是想见见外邦人出糗的模样罢了，他们中的某些家伙确实不太守规矩……
t_en: I just want to see outlanders make a fool of themselves. Some among them like to test the law's patience...

t_ch: 就拿我自己来说吧。我有一家面包店，平时也赚不了几个钱，勉强能维持生活。
t_en: Take me, for example. I have a bakery, it doesn't earn much most days, but I can make ends meet.

t_ch: 大概是从上上周开始，总有些外邦人在打烊前坐在店门口，希望我把当天没卖出去的剩面包施舍给他们。
t_en: It started about two weeks ago — there would be outlanders standing by the entrance when I closed up, hoping that I would offer any unsold bread to them.

t_ch: 我本来觉得这没什么，但有些买得起面包的公民知道之后，也过来占我的便宜，搞得人人都想吃免费的剩面包，我的生意都变差了。
t_en: I didn't think much of it at first, but the people who could afford them caught wind of this, and also came by to take advantage of my kindness. Everyone ended up wanting free bread, and my business suffered.

t_ch: 听起来也不是外邦人的错…
t_en: Doesn't sound like the outlanders were at fault...

t_ch: 怎么不是？要不是他们带了坏头，奥赫玛人怎么会这么贪心！
t_en: Are they not? If they hadn't started the trend, Okhemans wouldn't have learned to be so greedy!

t_ch: 你不给那些公民不就行了？
t_en: Couldn't you have just not offered any to those citizens?

t_ch: 这…我要是不给，大家又会说我吝啬，给外邦人不给自家人，生意不就更差了吗！
t_en: I... If I didn't, they would just call me miserly, offering bread to outlanders but refusing my countrymen... Business would be even worse then!

t_ch: 真离谱，这帮人就不能找个工作、自己掏钱买面包吗？
t_en: Outrageous. Can't these guys just find a job and pay for their own bread?

t_ch: 哎呀，这群外邦人，一个个那么勤奋干什么？居然还跑过来给大地兽免费打工……
t_en: Bah, what're these outlanders doing, being so hardworking? They're even willing to work for the dromases free of charge...

t_ch: 看来工作还是不够饱和。
t_en: Looks like there's not enough work to go around.

t_ch: 工作？可别提工作了！我正为这事发愁呢。
t_en: Work? Don't even mention that word! I'm tearing my hair out because of it.

t_ch: 勤奋不是好事吗？
t_en: Being hardworking is a bad thing?

t_ch: 它对公民来说也许是美德，但出现在外邦人身上，就只剩下麻烦了。
t_en: It may be a virtue to citizens, but with the outlanders showing up, it's spelled nothing but trouble.

t_ch: 我也想给大地兽搓背…
t_en: I also want to massage the back of a dromas...

t_ch: …你这是爱好异于常人，不像那群外邦人，纯属精力过剩。
t_en: ...You just have an odd hobby that raises some brows. That's nothing like those outlanders who're brimming with vigor.

t_ch: 之前来到圣城的外邦人数量不多，元老院就同意他们按批次进城。没想到这帮家伙…都太能干了。他们找工作只有两个要求，包吃、包住，连工钱都不需要付。
t_en: There weren't many outlanders who came to the holy city in the past, so the Council of Elders agreed to let them enter in batches. But who would've thought that these ruffians... were so exceptional. They had only two conditions while looking for a job: Food and lodging. You didn't even have to pay for their work.

t_ch: 有这么划算的伙计，现在基本没几个摊主愿意雇佣像我这样的本地人了——我可受不了不要工钱的活！
t_en: No business owner in their right mind would want to hire any locals with such affordable staff! I can't work for no pay!

t_ch: 要不你去城墙外找工作？
t_en: Why don't you look for a job beyond the walls?

t_ch: 啥意思？外面全是荒原，还有疯王眷属，有去无回啊！我没疯到那种程度。
t_en: What'd you mean? It's a wasteland out there, and with the Mad King's Kin roaming about, there's no return from that! I'm not THAT desperate.

t_ch: 为什么不开设新的岗位呢？
t_en: Why don't you create a new stall?

t_ch: 说得轻巧。市场总共就那么大，哪儿还能容下更多摊位了？
t_en: Easier said than done. The market's only so big, how many more stalls can it hold?

t_ch: 我能理解你。
t_en: That is true.

t_ch: 是吧？公民们应该团结起来维护自己的权益才对，不过这又说来话长了……
t_en: Right? The people should be banding together to protect their own rights, but that's another long story...

t_ch: 反正元老院已经在讨论如何处理外邦人的问题了，等官方通告出来再说也不迟。
t_en: Anyway, the Council of Elders is already discussing the outlander problem, we can wait for official word before acting.

t_ch: 那位老者真是舌灿莲花。拉莱德不是第一次做局刁难外邦人了，没想到这回给自己碰了一鼻子灰，哈哈~
t_en: That old man sure has a silver tongue. It's not the first time Liluder has tried to make things difficult for outlanders. Well, that backfired on him this time, haha!

t_ch: 等下轮抽签结束后，他怕不是还要折腾好一阵，到时可就又有乐子看了。
t_en: When the next drawing of lots ends, he'll probably still be struggling for a while — that'll be a funny sight to see then.

t_ch: 抽签？什么抽签？
t_en: Drawing lots? What lots?

t_ch: 抽签进城嘛。圣城已经收留了很多人，住房和其他资源都有限，尤其是在尼卡多利袭击之后。
t_en: To enter the city. The holy city has taken in many people, and accommodation and other resources are limited, especially after Nikador's attack.

t_ch: 这种局面当然不能来者不拒，所以外邦人想进入圣城，必须在抽签中获得资格才行。
t_en: We can't turn them away of course, so if the outlanders wish to enter the city, they'll have to draw lots to qualify.

t_ch: 不过缇宝大人前些日子带回了一队雅努斯的祭司，不用抽签就直接进城了…特殊时期，人才总是最珍贵的啊。
t_en: But Lady Tribbie brought back a group of Janus priests some days ago, and they didn't have to draw lots to enter the city... Talented individuals are a precious commodity in times of need.

t_ch: 缇宝他们不是带来了一批吗？
t_en: Didn't Tribbie bring back a group?

t_ch: 嗐，那里面有雅努斯的祭司，圣城需要他们的协助。特殊时期，人才总是最珍贵的嘛。
t_en: Those are Janus priests, the holy city needs their help. Talented individuals are a precious commodity in times of need.

t_ch: 圣城已经收留了很多人，住房和其他资源都有限，尤其是在尼卡多利袭击之后。这事儿指不定会引起多少微词。
t_en: The holy city has taken in many people, and accommodation and other resources are limited, especially after Nikador's attack. This matter will surely invite plenty of criticism.

t_ch: 你也是乐子人？
t_en: You're also looking to revel in others' misery?

t_ch: 别误会，我可不是什么嗜血观众。只是他这么喜欢做局，不看个戏总觉得吃亏。
t_en: Please don't misunderstand, I am no onlooker baying for blood. I just reckon it'd be a waste not to witness a troublemaker get his just deserts.

t_ch: 他倒也不是没有支持者——圣城的住房和其他资源都有限，尼卡多利的袭击更是雪上加霜。现在外邦人只能抽签进城。
t_en: He has his fair share of supporters — the holy city has limited space and resources, and Nikador's assault has only made that distinction more stark. Now, the outlanders have been forced to draw lots for entry.

t_ch: 不过缇宝大人前些日子带回了一队雅努斯的祭司，不用抽签就直接进城了…特殊时期，人才总是最珍贵的啊。
t_en: But Lady Tribbie brought back a group of Janus priests some days ago, and they didn't have to draw lots to enter the city... Talented individuals are a precious commodity in times of need.

t_ch: 现在城内的局面非常复杂，谁都能说出一堆道理来。公民们吵得厉害，元老院估计一时也拿不定主意。
t_en: The situation in the city is complicated. Everyone seems to have their fair share of arguments. The citizens are locked in their bickering, and the Council of Elders will likely reserve judgment for now.

t_ch: 给你一个忠告吧：学会独立思考是很重要的——就像我一样。
t_en: A word of advice: Learning to think for yourself is essential — just like me.

t_ch: 是你啊，小友。快下来吧，这里的水温刚刚好。
t_en: It's you, young friend. Come on down, the water is just right.

t_ch: 我对局势已经了如指掌！
t_en: I've got a complete grasp of the situation!

t_ch: 是嘛，那你的行动力着实令人佩服。
t_en: Is that so? Your actions are worth commending then.

t_ch: 但曾有贤者说过这样一句话：「舆论不能只用一边耳朵倾听。」不如也来听听我所掌握的信息吧。
t_en: There was once a sage who said this: "Public opinion cannot be heard with only one ear." How about listening to what I have gathered?

t_ch: 我能不下去吗？
t_en: Can I not go down?

t_ch: 我习惯一边感受水流，一边思考。有时我们距离真理只差这一点小小的灵感。你也该试一试。
t_en: I'm used to thinking with the flow of water. Sometimes we are but a miniscule spark of inspiration away from the truth. You ought to give it a try too.

t_ch: 只有超高温浴池才能满足我。
t_en: Only a hot bath will satisfy me.

t_ch: …泡澡这种事，讲究一个量力而行。我老了，太刺激的吃不消。请你先将就一下吧。
t_en: ...A bath is all about knowing your limits. Thrills aren't my strong suit at my age, please bear with me for a moment.

t_ch: 那我就不客气了。
t_en: Then I'll gladly accept it.

t_ch: 你进入了浴池——如他所说，水温刚刚好。
t_en: You enter the bath — as he said, the temperature is just right.

t_ch: 该从哪里说起呢…就从你开始吧。听说你初入奥赫玛时，尼卡多利的眷属正在城中肆虐。
t_en: Where should I begin... How about we start with you? I hear that Nikador's brethren were running rampant in the city when you first arrived in Okhema.

t_ch: 早在那场袭击发生之前，就偶尔有逃难的外邦人来这里寻求庇护。他们的城邦已经毁灭，仅凭自己没法在荒原上长期生存。
t_en: Before that attack happened, an occasional outlander seeking refuge would come here for shelter. Their cities had been razed to the ground, and they could not survive long in the wasteland.

t_ch: 而现在，希望得到圣城庇护的逃难者数量激增——因为你们击败了尼卡多利，通向奥赫玛的道路也变得安全了许多……
t_en: And now, the number of refugees seeking protection in the holy city is rising. Because you defeated Nikador, and made the journey to Okhema that much safer...

t_ch: 开拓者立大功。
t_en: The Trailblazers garner much merit.

t_ch: 哈哈~如果有哪个外邦的吟游诗人听了说你的壮举，我相信他会为你专门写一首歌的。
t_en: Haha! If an outlander bard hears of your exploits, I reckon he will surely write a song regaling your feat.

t_ch: 那这事还有我的锅？
t_en: You mean I caused this problem?

t_ch: 没必要这么想，未来之事本就不可预料。而且对那些外邦人来说，这是十足的好消息。
t_en: Don't think of it like that. Future events cannot be predicted. And to these outlanders, that is most welcome news.

t_ch: 真是残酷…
t_en: How cruel...

t_ch: 没错，「生存」是一件奢侈品。失去了城邦的人们在黑潮面前…没有胜算。
t_en: Exactly. Survival is a luxury. The homeless refugees who face the black tide... they have zero chance at victory.

t_ch: 是不是因为没交买路钱？
t_en: Is it because they didn't pay the toll?

t_ch: 并非谁都稀罕财富，尤其是那些失去神智的怪物…至少你的笑话让气氛没那么沉重了。
t_en: Not everyone covets wealth, especially monsters that have long forsaken their sanity... But your joke has injected some levity in such dire times.

t_ch: （保持沉默）
t_en: (Remain silent)

t_ch: 灾厄中的资源有限，奥赫玛没法接纳太多外邦人，来者不拒只会让城邦陷入混乱。
t_en: Resources during disasters are scarce, and Okhema can't take in too many outlanders. Welcoming everyone will only plunge the city into chaos.

t_ch: 如今，这些外邦人大多被安置在了远离城墙的避难所里，生存条件比荒原好上许多，还有圣城守卫保护。但那毕竟没法给他们家园的安定感。
t_en: Right now, most of these outlanders have been situated in a shelter far away from the city walls. The conditions are much better than the wasteland's, with the city guards protecting the place. But it will never grant them a sense of security akin to their homes.

t_ch: 元老院想出了一个权宜之策：只放一小部分外邦人进城，具体人选通过抽签决定。但反对者说，这种抽签可能并不公平，因为元老们可以故意挑选「有用」的人……
t_en: So the Council of Elders came up with a temporary solution: To allow only a small number of outlanders into the city, decided by drawing lots. This method may be unfair to those who object, because the Council has rigged the process to select the useful ones...

t_ch: 那些掌握特殊技能、擅长做粗重活计的强壮男子很容易得到机会；而带着孩子的老人则很难，即便他们实际上更需要帮助。
t_en: Strong men who have mastery over special skills and proficiency in heavy labor stand the highest chances, while old folk with children in tow the lowest, even if they require more assistance.

t_ch: 听上去的确不公平。
t_en: Sounds hardly fair.

t_ch: 可惜，没有证据表明这种筛选真的存在。甚至还有公民认为，那不过是东道主的正当权利——我们有资格决定帮谁。
t_en: Alas, there is no evidence to prove that this sorting really exists. Even the citizens think that this is the authority of the host city — we have the right to decide who we help.

t_ch: 这不是很正常吗？
t_en: Isn't this normal then?

t_ch: 的确有公民认为，这种筛选不过是东道主的正当权利——我们有资格决定帮谁。
t_en: Some citizens do think that these conditions are but the authority of the host city — we have the right to decide who we help.

t_ch: 那都别放进来了，最公平。
t_en: How about no one gets allowed in then, that's most fair.

t_ch: 某些激进者确实这么想，说奥赫玛不应接纳任何外邦人。但那样的话，我们在黑潮面前就会孤立无援了——万敌和白厄两位勇士也来自外邦。
t_en: Some radicals do think this way, saying that Okhema should not accept any outlanders at all. But if that were the case we would be solitary in our stand against the black tide — Mydei and Phainon are foreign as well.

t_ch: 原来元老院也是强度党。
t_en: So the Council of Elders recognizes strength as well.

t_ch: 你的意思是…我明白了，有能力的人确实容易受到优待。
t_en: You mean... I see, those of ability are more easily favored.

t_ch: 奥赫玛的资源有限，但也足够为绝大部分外邦人提供援助。真正的问题在于：<color=#dbc291ff>我们愿意付出代价吗？</color>
t_en: Okhema's resources are limited, but there is enough to aid most of the outlanders. The real question is: <color=#dbc291ff>Are we willing to pay the price?</color>

t_ch: 假设城门口来了一群快要饿死的陌生人，只要一口吃食就能活下去。奥赫玛当然有能力救助他们，但代价是你我会少吃……
t_en: If a group of strangers was starving at the gates, and they could live with a mouthful of food, Okhema would be more than capable of saving them, but you and I would have one fewer bite...

t_ch: 少吃一个苹果。对，就苹果吧，我爱吃苹果。「每天一苹果，医生远离我」。
t_en: ...One fewer bite of an apple, for example. I like apples. "An apple a day keeps the doctor away."

t_ch: 那么，<color=#dbc291ff>你愿意用自己桌上的一个苹果，去换陌生人的一条生路吗？</color>
t_en: Then, <color=#dbc291ff>are you willing to relinquish an apple on your table, in exchange for a stranger's survival?</color>

t_ch: 救人一命，少吃几个我也愿意。
t_en: I'd be willing to forgo any number of apples to save a life.

t_ch: 啊，多么高尚的回答，我就知道你有着少见的善良灵魂…但我们不能强迫所有公民都做出这样的选择。
t_en: Ah, a most noble answer. I knew you possessed a precious kind soul... but we cannot force all the citizens to make the same choice.

t_ch: 如果只是一个，那我愿意。
t_en: If it was just one, I'll be willing.

t_ch: 啊，看来你的心中的确存在着善。割让自身的利益——无论多么微小，其背后的高尚都值得赞扬…但我们不能强迫所有公民都这么选。
t_en: Ah, looks like kindness does indeed exist within you. To forgo one's benefits, no matter how small they may be, is still a noble act worth commending... But we cannot force all the citizens to do the same.

t_ch: 我只在利益不受损时愿意。
t_en: I'll only agree to acts that don't harm my own interests.

t_ch: 理解。只有当行善的代价可以承受时，人们心中的善才会被激发出来。
t_en: Understood. 'Tis only when the price for a kind act is bearable, that our benevolence is roused.

t_ch: 这得看我能从他那里换来什么。
t_en: That would depend on what they'd have in exchange.

t_ch: 很遗憾，快要饿死的人往往没什么财产…除了他们自己。
t_en: Alas, famished refugees would have a dearth of valuables on them... apart from their own lives.

t_ch: 哪怕没有代价，我也不愿意。
t_en: I wouldn't do it, even if there was no price.

t_ch: 嗯，这也是很多普通人的真实看法，而城邦的命运就是掌握在这些人手上的。
t_en: Yes, this is how most ordinary people truly feel, and the fate of the city lies in the hands of those who think like that.

t_ch: 对这个问题的回答造就了一切分歧。时至今日，公民们依旧为此争论不休。
t_en: The answers to this question incurred further discord. The city's people are locked in verbal conflict to this day.

t_ch: 至少在我看来，奥赫玛应当是包容的。哪怕我们不能拯救所有人，也应怀抱更多善意、给予他们足以活下去的资源……
t_en: How I see it at least, is that Okhema should be tolerant. Even if we can't save everyone, we should embrace kindness, and provide resources for others to live on...

t_ch: 因为真正的资源，是人。没有人的城邦就像没有秘酿的容器，难以在狂风中保持挺立。
t_en: Because the true resource is people. A city without her people is like a vessel without fine brew — unable to stand steadfast in violent gales.

t_ch: 我同意你的观点。
t_en: I agree with your perspective.

t_ch: 我不同意你的观点。
t_en: I disagree with your perspective.

t_ch: 对此我持保留意见。
t_en: I reserve judgment on this.

t_ch: 谁知道呢？这一切恐怕只有{RUBY_B#时间的流逝}欧洛尼斯的神迹{RUBY_E#}才能给出答案吧。
t_en: Who knows? I fear only {RUBY_B#Passing of Time}Oronyx's Miracle{RUBY_E#} may be able to give an answer.

t_ch: 在你们高声交谈时，一位青年悄悄来到了浴池旁边。
t_en: During your loud discourse, a youngster has surreptitiously sidled to the edge of the bath.

t_ch: 吾师，你怎么在这儿？真是让我好找…我有消息要告诉你。
t_en: Master, what are you doing here? I had such a hard time finding you... I have news for you.

t_ch: 是你啊，帕拉滕。我要先听好消息。
t_en: Oh, it's you, Palaten. I will have the good news first.

t_ch: 很可惜，只有坏消息——不久前，城门口来了一群外邦人。他们没有尝试进城，而是直接往命运重渊去了，现在不知情况如何。
t_en: Unfortunately, I have only bad tidings. An outlander group arrived at the city gates not long ago. They did not attempt to enter the city, their course set directly for the Abyss of Fate instead. Their circumstances are unknown right now.

t_ch: 苏克提瑞斯沉默了片刻，仿佛在思考一道由弟子抛出的谜题。
t_en: Soctires is silent for a moment, as if contemplating a conundrum that his student threw his way.

t_ch: 命运重渊…那边不是还很危险吗？他们为什么不去元老院的避难所？
t_en: The Abyss of Fate... Isn't it extremely dangerous there? Why did they not head for the Council of Elders' shelter?

t_ch: 不知道。可能是他们与奥赫玛存在信仰分歧——就像那位诺杜斯祭司一样。
t_en: I don't know. Perhaps they have a conflict of faith with Okhema — like that priest of Noldus.

t_ch: 正巧小友在这，我们不如亲自去看看情况…能麻烦你陪我们跑一趟吗？
t_en: Good thing you're here, young friend. How about we survey the situation personally... Might I trouble you to come with us?

t_ch: 乐意效劳。
t_en: I'm happy to help.

t_ch: 小友果然爽快。等回到奥赫玛之后，我请你吃饭。
t_en: You are most forthright. I must treat you to a meal when we return to Okhema.

t_ch: 为什么是我？
t_en: Why me?

t_ch: 通往命运重渊的路并不安全。我和帕拉滕只是普通学者，遇上麻烦不好脱身。
t_en: The road to the Abyss of Fate is not safe. Palaten and I are just ordinary scholars — we wouldn't be able to extricate ourselves from trouble.

t_ch: 而你是和黄金裔并肩作战的英雄。有你在，我们才能平安归来——等这件事结束之后，我请你吃饭。
t_en: And you are a hero who fought alongside the Chrysos Heirs. We will return safely with your company. I must treat you to a meal when we return to Okhema.

t_ch: 那可不能白跑。
t_en: It can't be a wasted trip.

t_ch: 哈哈，当然——等回到奥赫玛之后，我请你吃饭。
t_en: Haha, of course. I must treat you to a meal when we return to Okhema.

t_ch: 而且我相信，经过这次接触，我们对外邦人的了解也会更进一步。
t_en: And I believe that with this interaction, we understand the outlanders' plight better.

t_ch: 我能不去吗？
t_en: Can I not go?

t_ch: 我一直认为，眼见胜过耳闻。想要了解外邦人的处境，没什么比亲自去看看更客观了。
t_en: I have always thought that seeing is believing. There's nothing more convincing than personally witnessing the outlanders' circumstances to learn about them.

t_ch: 如果你实在对他们不感兴趣，也可以把我的邀请当作一份委托——等回到奥赫玛之后，我请你吃饭。
t_en: If you're really uninterested in them, you can treat my invite as a commission — I must treat you to a meal when we return to Okhema.

t_ch: 我准备好了，吾师。我们这便出发吧。
t_en: I'm ready, Master. Let us set off.

t_ch: 吾师，快看！那些东西……
t_en: Master, look! Those things...

t_ch: 疯王的眷属果然没那么容易退去。帕拉滕，你留在安全的地方；小友，待会我的身后就拜托你了。
t_en: Though expected, the Mad King's Kin are admittedly resilient. Palaten, you stay in a safe place. Young friend, I'll be counting on you to watch my back.

t_ch: 交给我了。
t_en: Will do.

t_ch: 哦哦哦，如此热血的台词！
t_en: Ooooh! That line's got me all fired up!

t_ch: 不保证存活…
t_en: I can't guarantee our survival...

t_ch: 吾师…原来你还懂武技的吗？
t_en: Master... you knew martial arts all along?

t_ch: 不，我只是趁机把那些难民组织起来，以免他们过于惊慌……
t_en: No, I simply seized the chance to rally those refugees, so they would keep calm...

t_ch: 啊啊啊！离我远点，怪物！
t_en: Aahhh! Get away from me, monsters!

t_ch: 就是现在！
t_en: Now!

t_ch: 咳，咳！
t_en: Ahem!

t_ch: 吾师，这些人…他们的状态很不好，在我们来之前就已经有人受伤了。
t_en: Master, these people... they're not doing well. Some were already injured before we got here.

t_ch: 的确，而且看样子，他们此前也没吃过像样的饭——这里的话事人在哪？
t_en: Indeed. And from the looks of them, they haven't had a proper meal for a while. Who's in charge here?

t_ch: 在、在这里……
t_en: H—Here...

t_ch: 一个带孩子的女人从人群中站了出来。
t_en: A woman holding a child emerges from the crowd.

t_ch: 多谢你们，还有这位灰发的英雄。我们…我们该怎么回报你们？
t_en: Thank you all so much, especially this gray-haired hero. How... How can we ever repay you?

t_ch: 先不说这个，你们为什么不去圣城指定的避难所？那里有卫兵驻守，比这里安全得多。
t_en: That can wait. Why didn't you go to the holy city's designated shelter? There are sentries garrisoned there. It's much safer than here.

t_ch: 避难所…卫兵……
t_en: Shelter... Guards...

t_ch: 之前我听见卫兵说什么「大地兽工坊骚乱」、「抽签」、「物资分配」之类的，就没敢上去搭话。
t_en: I overheard the guards before, talking about some "dromas workshop incident," "drawing lots," "resource allocation" and whatnot, so I didn't dare ask them to clarify.

t_ch: 我想，或许圣城的负担已经够大了。就算恳求卫兵放我们进城，也不过是给他们平添麻烦，最后闹个不痛快罢了。
t_en: I think the burden on the holy city is already heavy enough. Even if we beg the guards to let us in, it would only add to their troubles, and in the end, it would just lead to an unpleasant situation.

t_ch: 既然如此，我们不如先在附近找个地方落脚，看看能不能自食其力。只是没想到……
t_en: If that's the case, we might as well look for a nearby place to rest our feet, to see if we could fend for ourselves. But who would've thought...

t_ch: 妈妈，我们会死吗？
t_en: Mama, will we die?

t_ch: 不会的，宝贝…有妈妈在就不会。
t_en: No, baby... It won't happen as long as mama is around.

t_ch: 可是安东尼叔叔，还有爱莎姐姐…他们好像真的快要死了。
t_en: But Uncle Anthony, and Sister Aisha... They really look like they're going to die.

t_ch: 我的肚子也有些饿——之前不是说过，到了圣城就可以吃上饱饭了吗？妈妈，我们什么时候才能到圣城？
t_en: My tummy is hungry too... Didn't you say that we'd have something to eat once we got to the holy city? Mama, when will we get there?

t_ch: 对不起，孩子，对不起……
t_en: I'm sorry, darling. I'm sorry...

t_ch: 女人蹲下，紧紧抱住了自己的孩子，泪水从她眼角默默滑落。
t_en: The woman crouches and holds her child tight, tears silently streaming down the corners of her eyes.

t_ch: 苏克提瑞斯轻咳两声，把你们拽到了一旁。
t_en: Soctires softly clears his throat and pulls you to the side.

t_ch: 经历了这么多事，任谁都难免控制不住情绪。给那对母女留点空间吧。
t_en: Anyone who just suffered so much hardship would find it difficult to keep their emotions in check. Best give that mother and child some space.

t_ch: 这些外邦人的情况比想象中还要糟糕。如果放任不管的话，他们最多只能活一周——前提是没有更多袭击发生。
t_en: The outlanders' situation is much worse than I imagined. If we keep turning a blind eye, they will at best survive for a week. That is... if they aren't attacked by wild beasts first.

t_ch: 唉…真是人间惨剧啊。
t_en: *sigh* ...Such is the tragedy of life.

t_ch: 怎样才能帮到他们？
t_en: How can we help them?

t_ch: 想要直接带他们进城是很困难的，我们只能提供物质援助。对此，我有一个想法……
t_en: It would be hard to bring them directly into the city, so we can only provide supplies for them. And I have an idea about that...

t_ch: 我们救不了所有人…
t_en: We can't save everyone...

t_ch: 但至少我们可以帮助眼前的人，不是吗？我已经想到了一个办法……
t_en: But we can at least help those in front of us, can't we? I already have an idea...

t_ch: 这是他们咎由自取。
t_en: They brought this on themselves.

t_ch: 在发表评价之前，务必记得，不是所有人拥有选择。
t_en: Before you pass judgment, please remember that not everyone has a choice.

t_ch: 类似的悲剧在这片土地上发生过太多次，难道每个可怜人都是咎由自取吗？我看未必。
t_en: Similar tragedies have happened too many times in this land. Did all of them deserve their fates? I think not.

t_ch: 这些外邦人已经挺过了最艰难的旅程，只要一点简单的帮助就可以活下去。对此，我有一个想法……
t_en: The worst of the journey is over for these outlanders. They just need a little help to live on. I have an idea...

t_ch: （保持沉默）
t_en: (Remain silent)

t_ch: 至少这些外邦人已经挺过了最艰难的旅程，如今只要一点简单的帮助就可以活下去。对此，我有一个想法……
t_en: At least the worst of the journey is over for these outlanders. Now they just need some simple help to live on. I have an idea...

t_ch: 圣城的避难所距离城墙只有半天脚程，那里有卫兵守护，相对安全。只是凭这些人现在的状态很难抵达。
t_en: The holy city's shelter is only a half-day trek away from the city walls. There are guards there, so it'll be safer. It's just that the way these refugees are now... getting there would be difficult.

t_ch: 眼下他们没有多少食物，又因为刚才的袭击而急需药物。我们可以暂时为他们提供这两样东西，等伤员痊愈之后，再把他们送去避难所。
t_en: They haven't much food to begin with, and that last attack has left them in urgent need of emergency medical supplies. We can provide them with these two for now. When their wounded are healed, we can escort them to the shelter.

t_ch: 可是，吾师…药品在奥赫玛是战略物资，现在管控严格，一般公民很难买到的。
t_en: But, teacher... All medicine is now considered wartime supplies in Okhema — distribution is under strict control. Most people won't even be able to buy any.

t_ch: 我知道，我会想办法的…至少能确保他们活着。
t_en: I know. I will think of a way... at least to guarantee their survival.

t_ch: 好消息是，食物还没有受到管控。在<color=#dbc291ff>云石市集</color>随便就能买到足够这些人吃一周的粮食。
t_en: The good news is, food isn't strictly regulated. You can easily get enough food to feed these people for a week at the <color=#dbc291ff>Marmoreal Market</color>.

t_ch: 所以，我提议兵分两路：我和帕拉滕去找药，小友去市场买些喜欢的吃食回来，我们之后在这里集合。
t_en: So, I suggest we split up: Palaten and I will secure the medicine, while our young friend buys some food back, after which we will reconvene here.

t_ch: 哎，必须要我同行吗？原本还打算和这位灰发英雄一块选购来着。
t_en: Hey, do I really need to come along? I was thinking of doing the procurement with this gray-haired hero.

t_ch: 我需要一个帮手。记得我上个月嘱咐过的吗？我老了，很多事都必须有人拉一把才行。
t_en: I need a helper. Remember my instructions last month? I'm getting old, and there are many things that I need help for.

t_ch: 要是哪天我没力气走下去了，你还得接过我的衣钵，继续教化奥赫玛人民。
t_en: If there comes a day when I haven't the strength to walk a step farther, you'll have to take my mantle and carry on guiding the people of Okhema.

t_ch: 谨、谨遵师命……
t_en: A—As you wish, teacher...

t_ch: 那好。出发之前，小友还有什么想说的吗？
t_en: Well then, before we set out, is there anything our young friend wants to say?

t_ch: 包在我身上吧。
t_en: Leave it to me.

t_ch: 谢谢你，小友…真是麻烦你陪我跑上跑下了。等这件事结束后，我一定会认真向你道谢的。
t_en: Thank you, young friend... I really appreciate you accompanying me on my errands. When this is all over, I have to thank you in earnest.

t_ch: 不是说要请{F#她}{M#他}吃饭来着吗？别忘了算我一个。
t_en: Didn't you say you were going to treat {F#her}{M#him} to a meal? Don't forget my share.

t_ch: 回头记得给我报销。
t_en: Don't forget to reimburse me then.

t_ch: 哈哈，当然！等事情结束后，直接把账单交给帕拉滕就好，他最会算账了。
t_en: Haha, of course! When everything's over, just give the invoice to Palaten — he's the best at doing the accounts.

t_ch: 吾师，会算账不意味着要还账…别坑我啊！
t_en: Teacher, being good at the ledger isn't the same as paying for stuff... Please don't tease me!

t_ch: 不能向官方反映吗？
t_en: Can't we notify the officials?

t_ch: 人命关天，已经没时间走流程了…等问题解决之后，再向元老院报备吧。
t_en: Lives are at stake. We've got no time to do it by the book... We'll report this to the Council of Elders when the problem is resolved.

t_ch: 听起来这些人的命运全靠我们了。没想到我一介学徒，居然也有当英雄的一天…想想还有点激动呢！
t_en: It sounds like these people's fates are relying on our success. I never thought a lowly apprentice like me would get a chance to perform such heroics... Sounds awfully exciting!

t_ch: 这些人关我们什么事？
t_en: What do these people have to do with us?

t_ch: 此言差矣。我们总是要给弱者提供力所能及的帮助，不只是因为道德，哪怕是从功利角度来说，也应当因此。
t_en: You're asking the wrong question. We should always provide all the help we can to the weak, not just because it is moral. It is the correct action, even from a utilitarian perspective.

t_ch: 说不定什么时候，强与弱的身份就会发生转换，我们也会需要他们的帮助…而且，刻法勒的伟大之处，不正是在于替众人背负世界吗。
t_en: Who knows when the roles of the strong and weak will be reversed? When we will also need their help? Besides, isn't Kephale's majesty in bearing the burdens of the world for the people?

t_ch: 没想到我一介学徒，居然也有机会继承刻法勒的精神，想想还有点激动呢！
t_en: I never thought a lowly apprentice like me would get the chance to carry on Kephale's will. It sounds very exciting!

t_ch: 那么，一会儿见了，灰发的英雄。
t_en: Then, we'll meet later, gray-haired hero.

t_ch: 妈妈，我不想再吃这些硬面包了，牙都快硌掉了！
t_en: Mama, I don't want to eat this rock-hard bread anymore. My teeth are going to fall out!

t_ch: 乖，再吃一口就好。下次我们不吃硬面包了，我保证……
t_en: Be a good girl and have one more bite. We won't have to eat hard bread next time, I promise...

t_ch: 好啊~等到了圣城，我要吃烤苹果派！
t_en: Yay! I want baked apple pie when we reach the holy city!

t_ch: 找个能落脚的地方怎么就这么难？我只是想过上安稳的生活而已……
t_en: Is there no recourse in our search for a safe haven? I simply wish to live out a peaceful, simple life...

t_ch: 难道最后还是要回北方？不，我可不想再走一遍了！
t_en: Do we have to return north in the end? No, I'm not making that trek again!

t_ch: 刻法勒在上，欧洛尼斯保佑，扎格列斯显灵…哪位都行，帮帮我们吧。
t_en: By Kephale, may Oronyx protect us, may Zagreus grace our presence... Any of them will do. Please help us.

t_ch: 啊…啊……
t_en: Ah... Agh...

t_ch: 我的腿…它还在吗？我有些…没知觉了……
t_en: My leg... Is it still there? I can't... seem to feel it...

t_ch: 你撑住啊。领队已经在想办法了，你很快就会好起来的…别睡，别睡！
t_en: Hang in there. The leader is already trying to help. You'll get better real soon... Don't go to sleep. Don't sleep!

t_ch: 好…好……
t_en: Okay... Okay...

t_ch: 这个商贩似乎是一夜间冒出来的，你不记得自己在集市上见过她。
t_en: This shopkeeper appears to have popped up overnight, since you don't remember seeing her at the market.

t_ch: 哟，欢迎光临维比娅的小店。能有您这样的英雄眷顾，我这摊位都蓬荜生辉了。
t_en: Ooh, welcome to Vibia's little shop. It's a great honor for a hero such as you to be gracing my humble store.

t_ch: 我要买一箱食物。
t_en: I want a crate of food.

t_ch: 食物？当然，我这就把压箱底的货都拿出来招待您。
t_en: Food? Of course. I'll have only my most premium goods brought out to you.

t_ch: 商贩从角落里拽来一个落满灰尘的木箱，掀开了盖子一角……
t_en: The shopkeeper drags out a dusty wooden crate from a corner, and opens a corner...

t_ch: 干瘪的蔬菜、诡异的肉类、硬如武器的面包…种种迹象表明，这箱食物是次品中的次品。
t_en: Shriveled vegetables, strange meats, bread hard as steel... All signs indicate that this crate of food is obviously inferior beyond all atrocity.

t_ch: 还没等你看清细节，商贩就重新把木箱拖回角落，用一块麻布罩住了。
t_en: Before you even get a glance at the intricacies, she drags the crate back to its corner and covers it with a cloth.

t_ch: 怎么样？昨天刚到的货，非常新鲜！
t_en: How's about it? They just arrived yesterday. Fresh as it gets!

t_ch: 你这货是从墓里挖来的？
t_en: From which grave did you dig that crate up?

t_ch: 什么话？这明明是我辛辛苦苦偷…我是说搜集来的，你可不能空口污人清白！
t_en: How could you? I spent all that effort steal... I mean, searching for it. You shouldn't accuse an innocent!

t_ch: 这一箱价钱也不贵，<unbreak>2000</unbreak>利衡币。想要的话就给钱。
t_en: It's not expensive either, only <unbreak>2,000</unbreak> Balance Coins. Cough up the coin if you want it.

t_ch: 你压箱底的就这货色？
t_en: This is your most premium produce?

t_ch: …这一箱价钱又不贵，才<unbreak>2000</unbreak>利衡币。买不了吃亏，买不了上当啊！
t_en: ...This crate doesn't cost much, only <unbreak>2,000</unbreak> Balance Coins. There's no way you're losing out on this deal!

t_ch: 怎么样，到底要不要？要就给钱。
t_en: How's about it? Want it? Cough up the coin then.

t_ch: （这家确实很便宜，但质量不敢恭维…如果我想用廉价货色打发那些外邦人，它会是不错的选择。）
t_en: (It is very cheap, but its quality remains suspect... It is a decent option, if I intend to use substandard food to get those outlanders off my back.)

t_ch: 我要了。
t_en: I'll take it.

t_ch: 哎呀，您果然识货。喏，我这儿还有些水果，一并送你当赠品好了。
t_en: Hah, I knew you had a good eye. Oh, I have some fruit here too, take 'em as a gift.

t_ch: 记得别让卫兵看见箱子，不然可能会有麻烦——那就不关我的事了。
t_en: Don't let the guards see the crate, or there'll be trouble — I won't be liable for any of that.

t_ch: 这是你最后一次看见这个商贩。在把货箱交给你之后，她便朝着远离市场的方向狂奔，直到消失在视野尽头。
t_en: This is the last time you see this peddler. After giving you the crate, she sprints off into the distance, far away from the market, and fades beyond the horizon.

t_ch: 你扛着货箱，一路回到了重渊。
t_en: You lug the crate back to the abyss.

t_ch: 我再看看。
t_en: I'll take another look around here.

t_ch: 别看了，再看也不会有区别的…唉，随你吧。
t_en: Forget that, it won't make a difference... Bah, it's your call.

t_ch: 考虑好了没有？过了这村没这店了！
t_en: Done thinking 'bout it? It's now or never!

t_ch: 我要了。
t_en: I'll take it.

t_ch: 哎呀，您果然识货。喏，我这儿还有些水果，一并送你当赠品好了。
t_en: Hah, I knew you had a good eye. Oh, I have some fruit here too, take 'em as a gift.

t_ch: 记得别让卫兵看见箱子，不然可能会有麻烦——那就不关我的事了。

t_en: This is the last time you see this peddler. After giving you the crate, she sprints off into the distance, far away from the market, and fades beyond the horizon.

t_en: You lug the crate back to the abyss.

t_ch: 我再看看。
t_en: I'll take another look around here.

t_ch: 别看了，再看也不会有区别的…唉，随你吧。
t_en: Forget that, it won't make a difference... Bah, it's your call.

t_ch: 又见面了，可爱的小家伙。有什么想吃的水果吗？
t_en: We meet again, my adorable friend. Any fruit you want to eat?

t_ch: 我要买一箱食物。
t_en: I want a crate of food.

t_ch: 不是蔬果，而是食物？呵呵，小朋友，你还真会给我出难题。
t_en: Not fruit and vegetables, but food? Heh-heh, you sure know how to create a quandary in me, little friend.

t_ch: 但这可难不倒我，稍等就好……
t_en: But this won't vex me at all. Just wait a while...

t_ch: 德墨忒尔笑着走进屋子。返回时，她手上多了几个巨大的竹篮——大到让你怀疑她是怎么搬动的。
t_en: Demetria laughs as she enters the house. When she returns, several large baskets are hanging off her arms — they are so big you wonder how she's even carrying them.

t_ch: 除蔬果以外，篮子里也包括了肉类和面包。至于质量，只能算是不好不坏。
t_en: Besides fruit and vegetables, there are also meats and bread in the baskets. As for their quality, you can only vouch for their mediocrity.

t_ch: 在向你展示过后，德墨忒尔将篮子放回了柜台后面，看来她也没强壮到能一直举着它们。
t_en: After parading them in front of you, Demetria sets the baskets back behind the counter. She is probably tired from having to carry them.

t_ch: 怎么样，小家伙？如果你想要，我的报价是<unbreak>4000</unbreak>利衡币。
t_en: What about it, little one? If you wish, I'll sell them to you for <unbreak>4,000</unbreak> Balance Coins.

t_ch: （这家店的价格和质量都适中。如果我想要无功无过的选择，那就是它了。）
t_en: (This shop's price and quality are acceptable. If I want a middling option, this is probably it.)

t_ch: 我要了。
t_en: I'll take it.

t_ch: 好。你要把这些食物带到哪儿去？我帮你送一程吧……
t_en: Okay. Where do you want the food delivered to? I can help you with that...

t_ch: 谢绝了老店长的帮助，你带着篮子一路回到了重渊。
t_en: After politely declining the shopkeeper's offer to help, you bring the baskets back to the abyss.

t_ch: 我再看看。
t_en: I'll take another look around here.

t_ch: 没事，你想看多久都可以。
t_en: No worries, take all the time you want.

t_ch: 怎么样，小家伙，做好决定了吗？
t_en: Little one, have you decided yet?

t_ch: 我要了。
t_en: I'll take it.

t_ch: 好。你要把这些食物带到哪儿去？我帮你送一程吧……
t_en: Okay. Where do you want the food delivered to? I can help you with that...

t_ch: 谢绝了老店长的帮助，你带着篮子一路回到了重渊。
t_en: After politely declining the shopkeeper's offer to help, you bring the baskets back to the abyss.

t_ch: 我再看看。
t_en: I'll take another look around here.

t_ch: 没事，你想看多久都可以。
t_en: No worries, take all the time you want.

t_ch: 您想来点什么呢？本地的特色美食，还是那些早已失传的外邦菜肴？
t_en: What would you like to eat? Local specialties? Or perhaps something more outlandish that has been lost to time?

t_ch: 我要买一箱食物。
t_en: I want a crate of food.

t_ch: 「一箱」食物？那只好把我的备餐拿出来秀一秀了……
t_en: A "crate" of food? That means I'll just have to flaunt my reserve stock then...

t_ch: 放心，这儿的食物都是奥赫玛最好的。就算不是最好，也是数一数二的。
t_en: Don't worry, the food here is Okhema's finest. Even if it's not, it's still pretty up there.

t_ch: 克瑞斯招招手，帮工们从房间里搬出几个敞口陶瓶，里面存放的食物——如他所说，令人垂涎。
t_en: Kyros waves his hand, and from the room, his helpers carry several ceramic vases containing food. It is as he said: They look delicious.

t_ch: 摆盘精美的果蔬、新鲜出炉的浓汤，散发着香气的秘酿…每样都是食物中的上品，并且经过细心烹饪。
t_en: Lush fruit and vegetables in a platter, aromatic soup fresh from the oven, fine brew emanating intoxicating fragrance... Each and every item is world-class, and made with culinary intricacy.

t_ch: 怎么样？我说过这是全奥赫玛最好的。当然，价格不算便宜，<unbreak>8000</unbreak>利衡币。
t_en: What'd you think? I told you I'd give you Okhema's finest. Of course, they cost quite a bit: <unbreak>8,000</unbreak> Balance Coins.

t_ch: （这家店的质量相当高，但是价钱也很贵。如果我想给外邦人吃顿好的，它会是不错的选择。）
t_en: (This shop's quality is top-notch, and its price reflects that. If I want to give the outlanders something sumptuous, this would be a fine choice.)

t_ch: 我要了。
t_en: I'll take it.

t_ch: 我看看…钱数没错。稍等，我帮你打包一下。
t_en: Let's see... This is the right amount. Hold on, I'll help you pack the food.

t_ch: 啊对了，冒昧问一句，你买这么多食物是要做什么？
t_en: Ah yes, if you don't mind me asking: Why are you buying so much food?

t_ch: 送给一伙快饿死的外邦人。
t_en: To feed a group of dying outlanders.

t_ch: 外邦人啊…怎么说呢，这些吃食还挺宝贵的，我希望它们的美味能被充分享用…你懂我意思吗？
t_en: Outlanders eh... How do I put this... This food is quite valuable, and I hope that their taste will be fully savored... You get my drift?

t_ch: 又不是不给你钱。
t_en: It's not like I'm not paying you.

t_ch: 话是这么说没错…但拜托你一定要让他们躺在椅子上、按步骤用餐，不然就享受不到百分百的美味了！
t_en: You may be right... but I would ask that you must have them lay back in their chairs and taste the food in order, or they won't be able to enjoy the utmost in flavors!

t_ch: 食物不就是给人吃的？
t_en: Isn't food for people to eat?

t_ch: 不不不，如果不能躺在椅子上、按照步骤用餐，食物的风味就会被浪费……
t_en: No no no, if they don't lay back in chairs and eat the meal in order, the food's flavor will go to absolute waste...

t_ch: 那我换一家。
t_en: I'll go to another shop then.

t_ch: 哎别，何必呢？我就是想说这些食物很珍贵而已，只要你躺在椅子上，按照步骤用餐就好……
t_en: Oh come on, there's no need for that. I just wanted to put emphasis on how priceless these foods are. Just make sure you lie back in your chair and savor them in order...

t_ch: 我自己吃，不行吗？
t_en: I'm eating them myself. How about that?

t_ch: 不不，没有不行，果然是英雄出少年啊。那你千万记得，要躺在椅子上、按照步骤用餐……
t_en: Of course! You're definitely a hero in the height of your youth. But you must remember to lie back in your chair and eat them in order...

t_ch: 你猜？
t_en: Take a guess.

t_ch: 总感觉有古怪…算了，那不是我该关心的事。但你千万记得，要躺在椅子上、按照步骤用餐……
t_en: Something feels off... No matter, it has nothing to do with me. But you must remember to lie back in your chair and eat them in order...

t_ch: 在克瑞斯关于进食规矩的科普中，你抱着装食物的容器跑远了。
t_en: As Kyros is rambling on fine dining practices, you've already left the vicinity, the vessels full of food bundled in your arms.

t_ch: 我再看看。
t_en: I'll take another look around here.

t_ch: 看得再多，你也找不到比我更好的。
t_en: You can look, but you won't find a better establishment than mine.

t_ch: 货比三家之后，总算意识到我所言非虚了吧？
t_en: After browsing the various shops, you must have realized that my words ring true, no?

t_ch: 我要了。
t_en: I'll take it.

t_ch: 我看看…钱数没错。稍等，我帮你打包一下。
t_en: Let's see... This is the right amount. Hold on, I'll help you pack the food.

t_en: To feed a group of dying outlanders.

t_en: Outlanders eh... How do I put this... This food is quite valuable, and I hope that their taste will be fully savored... You get my drift?

t_en: It's not like I'm not paying you.

t_en: You may be right... but I would ask that you must have them lay back in their chairs and taste the food in order, or they won't be able to enjoy the utmost in flavors!

t_en: Take a guess.

t_ch: 总感觉有古怪…算了，那不是我该关心的事。但你千万记得，要躺在椅子上、按照步骤用餐……
t_en: Something feels off... No matter, it has nothing to do with me. But you must remember to lie back in your chair and eat them in order...

t_en: As Kyros is rambling on fine dining practices, you've already left the vicinity, the vessels full of food bundled in your arms.

t_en: I'll take another look around here.

t_ch: 看得再多，你也找不到比我更好的。
t_en: You can look, but you won't find a better establishment than mine.

t_ch: 妈妈，我们什么时候到圣城？我要吃烤苹果派！
t_en: Mama, when will we reach the holy city? I wanna eat baked apple pie!

t_ch: 很快了，很快就到了……
t_en: Soon, we're almost there...

t_ch: 就在大概三秒钟后。
t_en: We'll get there in three seconds.

t_ch: 这不是那位灰发英雄嘛？难道……
t_en: Isn't that the gray-haired hero? Could you have...

t_ch: 给你们的，还不快道谢？
t_en: This is for you. How about a thank-you?

t_ch: 给我们的？难道……
t_en: For us? Could you have...

t_ch: 嘿嘿，圣城来咯。
t_en: Hee-hee, the holy city is here.

t_ch: 诶？你的意思是……
t_en: Huh? You mean...

t_ch: 你交出了落满灰尘的木箱，里面的菜叶似乎比之前更蔫了。
t_en: You hand over the dust-laden wooden crate — the leaves of the vegetables within look even more withered than before.

t_ch: 里面…全都是食物？
t_en: Inside this... It's all food?

t_ch: 妈妈，它们看上去好像不是很新鲜……
t_en: Mama, they don't look very fresh...

t_ch: 傻孩子，现在能有吃的就不错了，还奢求什么！
t_en: Silly child, having something to eat is a blessing in and of itself. You shouldn't be so picky!

t_ch: 多谢你为我们提供这些，英雄。我很快就会把它发给同伴们。
t_en: Thank you for supplying us with food, hero. I will distribute them to our companions quickly.

t_ch: 来，宝贝，你也跟这位{F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}道声谢吧。
t_en: Darling, say thank you to this kind {F#lady}{M#man}.

t_ch: 谢、谢谢{F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}……
t_en: Th—Thank you, kind {F#lady}{M#man}...

t_ch: 小女孩的目光在箱子上来回游弋，总算在箱子边缘找到了一个还算新鲜的苹果。
t_en: The little girl's eyes dart back and forth over the crate, finally coming across an apple that looks relatively fresh at the edge of the box.

t_ch: {F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}，这个新鲜的给你吃。
t_en: {F#Miss}{M#Mister}, this fresh apple is for you.

t_ch: 她皮笑肉不笑地捧着那个苹果，把它塞进你的怀里。
t_en: She offers the apple with a awkward grin, thrusting it into your arms.

t_ch: 傻孩子，人家怎么会稀罕一个苹果呢？再说这本来就是人家的东西……
t_en: Silly child, why would our benefactors be satisfied with just an apple? And it was theirs to begin with...

t_ch: 还不等她把话说完，一个人影便急匆匆跑了过来。母女二人识相地选择了回避。
t_en: Before she can finish her sentence, a figure runs over in a hurry. The mother and child tactfully excuse themselves.

t_ch: 你交出了那几个竹篮，里面的食物虽不算珍馐，却也是这些人最需要的。
t_en: You hand over the baskets. The food inside isn't exactly delicacies, but they are just what these people need.

t_ch: 里面…全都是食物？而且看上去品质还不错……
t_en: It's... all food inside? And they look pretty good too...

t_ch: 妈妈，苹果，是苹果！
t_en: Mama, apple, it's an apple!

t_ch: 是啊，今天我们可以做苹果馅饼了——虽然离烤苹果派还有些差距，但也不错。
t_en: Yes, honey. We can make apple tarts today... They're not baked apple pies, but they taste just as good.

t_ch: 多谢你为我们提供这些，英雄。我很快就会把它发给同伴们。
t_en: Thank you for supplying us with food, hero. I will distribute them to our companions quickly.

t_ch: 来，宝贝，你也跟这位{F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}道声谢吧。
t_en: Darling, say thank you to this kind {F#lady}{M#man}.

t_ch: 谢、谢谢{F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}。
t_en: Th—Thank you, kind {F#lady}{M#man}.

t_ch: 女孩抓起篮子里最大最新鲜的苹果，笑着塞到你的怀里。
t_en: The girl grabs the biggest and freshest apple in the basket and shoves it into your hands with a grin.

t_ch: {F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}，这个最大的给你吃。
t_en: {F#Miss}{M#Mister}, you should have the biggest one.

t_ch: 傻孩子，人家怎么会稀罕一个苹果呢？再说这本来就是人家的东西……
t_en: Silly child, why would our benefactors be satisfied with just an apple? And it was theirs to begin with...

t_ch: 还不等她把话说完，一个人影便急匆匆跑了过来。母女二人识相地选择了回避。
t_en: Before she can finish her sentence, a figure runs over in a hurry. The mother and child tactfully excuse themselves.

t_ch: 你交出了那几个敞口陶瓶，里面的食物无比精美、仿佛闪着金光。
t_en: You hand over the ceramic vases. The food in them is exquisite, as if radiating with flavorful light.

t_ch: 这…如此丰盛的食物，真是送给我们的？我不会在做梦吧……
t_en: This... such a bountiful spread, is this really for us? I'm not dreaming, am I...

t_ch: 妈妈，苹果派，是我最想吃的烤苹果派！
t_en: Mama, apple pie, it's the baked apple pie that I want!

t_ch: 傻孩子，这里比烤苹果派好吃的东西有许多，何必惦记那玩意。
t_en: Silly child, there's plenty of food more delicious than baked apple pie in here. Why fuss over a pie?

t_ch: 谢谢，谢谢您，您简直是我们的大英雄！我很快就会把这些发给同伴们。
t_en: Thank you, thank you, thank you! You are our hero and savior! I'll give them out to our companions right now.

t_ch: 您的这份恩情我们一定会回报——虽然暂时没钱，但如果有需要我们的地方，您尽管开口，我们随时乐意效劳。
t_en: We will definitely repay this favor. Though we have no money now, but if you ever have need of us, just give us the word, and we will be more than happy to help.

t_ch: 来，宝贝，你也跟这位{F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}道声谢吧。
t_en: Darling, say thank you to this kind {F#lady}{M#man}.

t_ch: 谢、谢谢{F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}！
t_en: Th—Thank you, kind {F#lady}{M#man}!

t_ch: 女孩抓起陶瓶里最大最新鲜的苹果，笑着塞到你的怀里。
t_en: The girl grabs the biggest and freshest apple in one of the vases and thrusts it into your hands with a smile.

t_ch: {F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}，这个最大的给你吃。
t_en: {F#Miss}{M#Mister}, you should have the biggest one.

t_ch: 如果不介意的话，您干脆留下来和我们共进佳肴吧~
t_en: If you don't mind, how about having a meal with us!

t_ch: 当你们沉浸在欢笑中时，一个人影急匆匆跑了过来。母女二人识相地选择了回避。
t_en: While everyone revels in their joy, a figure hurriedly rushes over. The mother and child tactfully excuse themselves.

t_ch: 不好了，英雄！吾师，吾师他，他…药，药！
t_en: Grave news, hero! Master, Master, h—he... med, medi—!

t_ch: 你先冷静一下…
t_en: Catch your breath first...

t_ch: 他出事了？
t_en: Is he in trouble?

t_ch: 我就知道他不让人省心！
t_en: I knew he was gonna be a handful!

t_ch: …切克闹？
t_en: ...Check it?

t_ch: …不要在这个时候念诵法吉娜的咒语啊！
t_en: ...Now's not the time to chant one of Phagousa's curses!

t_ch: 呼…呼…好，冷静了。你仔细听我说……
t_en: Fuu... Hooo... All right, I'm calm. You have to listen carefully...

t_ch: 最开始，吾师说自己有门路搞到药，但必须由我把药带出城。他为我指明一处小巷，让我在一刻钟后回来拿药……
t_en: In the beginning, Master said he had a way to get the medicine, but I had to be the one to transport it out of the city. He directed me to an alley, and told me to return to take the medicine from him when a quint had passed...

t_ch: 我看时间还早，就打算先去整点东西吃，结果等吃完都快到践行时了。我连忙冲出店面，往那条街跑……
t_en: There was still some time, so I decided to find something to eat, but when I was done, it was almost Action Hour. I dashed out of the shop and headed toward the alley...

t_ch: 没跑多久，我就看见吾师跟三个卫兵走在一起——虽然吾师没有戴上镣铐，但看表情就知道，他一定是被迫的。
t_en: I didn't get far before I saw Master walking with three guards. He wasn't cuffed, but I knew from his expression that he wasn't with them out of choice.

t_ch: 我一路跟踪那帮卫兵，发现他们居然把吾师押去了<color=#dbc291ff>云石天宫的上层</color>！那里人多眼杂，我没法靠得太近，只好回来找你商量对策。
t_en: I tailed the guards and found that they had detained Master on the <color=#dbc291ff>upper levels of Marmoreal Palace</color>! There were a lot of people there, and I couldn't get close, so I hurried back to discuss a plan with you.

t_ch: 哦对，之后我确实在那个小巷里找到了药，你说神奇不神奇？
t_en: Oh yes, I did find the medicine in that alley after all that. It was a curious turn of events.

t_ch: 你没被发现吧？
t_en: You weren't discovered?

t_ch: 肯定没有，不然我哪有机会来到这里？
t_en: I'm pretty sure I wasn't — I wouldn't have been able to return otherwise.

t_ch: 多亏吾师恰是时候地引开了卫兵的注意…你是和黄金裔并肩作战过的英雄，求你帮吾师做做解释吧，卫兵们肯定会听的！
t_en: It was fortunate that Master distracted the guards when he had the chance... You're the hero who fought alongside the Chrysos Heirs. Please, you have to explain Master's case to them. I'm sure the guards will listen!

t_ch: 有没有想过药是哪来的？
t_en: You ever thought about how the medicine got there?

t_ch: 吾师让我去小巷里拿药，然后他就被抓走了，难道说……
t_en: Master bade me retrieve the medicine from the alley, and then he was arrested by them. Don't tell me...

t_ch: 不，这里面肯定有什么误会…你是和黄金裔并肩作战过的英雄，求你帮吾师做做解释吧，卫兵们肯定会听的！
t_en: No, there must be some misunderstanding here... You're the hero who fought alongside the Chrysos Heirs. Please, you have to explain Master's case to them. I'm sure the guards will listen!

t_ch: 一看就是干了坏事。
t_en: He must've done something bad.

t_ch: 吾师不是穷凶极恶的罪犯！这里面肯定有什么误会……
t_en: Master is not some nefarious criminal! There must be some misunderstanding...

t_ch: 我是他的弟子，卫兵们肯定不会听我解释。但你是和黄金裔并肩作战过的英雄——求你帮吾师说说情吧！
t_en: I am his student, the guards will never listen to me. But you're the hero who fought alongside the Chrysos Heirs — please talk to the guards on behalf of Master!

t_ch: 说罢，帕拉滕便掏出药袋、向受伤的外邦人跑去……
t_en: With that said, Palaten takes out the medicine pouch and runs in the wounded outlanders' direction...

t_ch: 苏克提瑞斯被抓走了…不排除误会的可能，但还是跟他当面问清楚比较好。
t_en: Soctires has been captured... A misunderstanding's not beyond possibility, but it'd be better if I asked him in person.

t_ch: 帕拉滕说他被关在<color=#dbc291ff>云石天宫的上层</color>，应该不难找。
t_en: Palaten said he's being detained on the <color=#dbc291ff>upper floors of Marmoreal Palace</color>, so he shouldn't be hard to find.

t_ch: 啊…啊……
t_en: Ah... Agh...

t_ch: 我的腿…它还在吗？我有些…没知觉了……
t_en: My leg... Is it still there? I can't... seem to feel it...

t_ch: 你撑住啊，他们已经搞到药了，你很快就会好起来的…别睡，别睡！
t_en: Hang in there. They have the medicine. You'll get well in no time... Don't sleep!

t_ch: 忍着点，我这就帮你包扎伤口。
t_en: Bear with it. I'll help you bandage your wound.

t_ch: 好…好……
t_en: Okay... Okay...

t_ch: 站住，干什么的？
t_en: Hold it, what are you doing?

t_ch: 苏克提瑞斯在这里吗？
t_en: Is Soctires here?

t_ch: 不认识我了？是我杀了疯王！
t_en: Don't recognize me? I'm the one who slew the Mad King!

t_ch: 我来看看被你们关押的犯人。
t_en: I'm here to see the prisoner you detained.

t_ch: 原来是和两位黄金裔并肩作战的异邦英雄，失礼了。
t_en: It's the outlander hero who fought alongside the two Chrysos Heirs. My apologies.

t_ch: 作为贵客，您可以破例探视一小会儿。等时间到了，我会来通知您。
t_en: I can bend the rules for a short visit for an esteemed guest. I will inform you when time's up.

t_ch: 这个房间的布设相当舒适，没有铁栅栏或其他限制手段，为被关押者保留了最后的体面。
t_en: This room's layout is quite cozy. There are no bars nor restraining devices — a modicum of respect has been left for whoever gets detained here.

t_ch: 小友，你来了……
t_en: You're here, young friend...

t_ch: 苏克提瑞斯的眼神有些躲闪，似乎并不愿被你看到这副模样。
t_en: Soctires' eyes are somewhat avoidant, as if he is reluctant to have you see him in this state.

t_ch: 你都做了什么？
t_en: What did you do?

t_ch: 即便我不说，相信你也能猜到大概——为了帮外邦人找到药品，我溜进了圣城存放物资的仓库。
t_en: I believe you have already guessed the gist of it, even if I don't say anything — to help the outlanders secure medicine, I slipped into the holy city's supply warehouse.

t_ch: 帕拉滕说你被卫兵抓了。
t_en: Palaten said you were caught by the guards.

t_ch: 他没有说谎。至于被抓的原因，即便我不说，相信你也能猜到大概……
t_en: He wasn't lying. As for the reason I was caught, even if I said nothing, I'm sure you can guess the gist of it...

t_ch: 为了帮外邦人找到药品，我溜进了圣城存放物资的仓库。
t_en: To help the outlanders secure medicine, I slipped into the holy city's supply warehouse.

t_ch: 我本不想来的。
t_en: I didn't want to come initially.

t_ch: 是啊，谁会愿意跟嫌犯扯上关系呢——为了帮外邦人找到药品，我溜进了圣城存放物资的仓库。
t_en: Yes, who would want to be involved with a suspect... To help the outlanders secure medicine, I slipped into the holy city's supply warehouse.

t_ch: 这是偷窃，我很清楚自己在做非法之事。如今这番下场，也算是我咎由自取。
t_en: It is theft. I understand very well that I was committing a crime. As for how it ended, it was simply my just deserts.

t_ch: 但要说是否值得，我唯一的遗憾就是没能把更多药品运送出城、交给那些需要的人……
t_en: As to whether it was worth it, my only regret is that I didn't manage to transport more medicine out of the city into the hands of those who need it...

t_ch: 抱歉，我辜负了你们的期待。之前承诺说请你吃饭，大概也要食言了。
t_en: I'm sorry for disappointing you. I may have to renege on that promise to treat you to a meal.

t_ch: 当时一定有更好的办法。
t_en: There must have been a better way.

t_ch: 是啊，作为旁观者，我们当然可以拿出一万个理由说服自己：只要再等等，总会有更好的办法出现。
t_en: Of course. As an onlooker, we can always find myriad reasons to convince ourselves, that a better idea would surface if we just waited a while longer.

t_ch: 但那些需要帮助的人等不了，我也等不了——我是个老人，老人知道自己不剩多少时间，所以也没多少耐心。
t_en: But those who need help can't wait, and neither could I. I am an old man — I don't have the luxury of time nor patience.

t_ch: 有必要做到这种地步吗？
t_en: Was there a need to go this far?

t_ch: 不止你有这份疑惑：作为公民，为什么我要冒着失去一切的风险，去帮助一群陌生人呢？
t_en: You're not the only one with this question: As a citizen, why did I risk everything to help a bunch of strangers?

t_ch: 因为我也希望在陷入困境时，能有人来拉我一把。
t_en: Because I, too, hope that in my time of need, someone will lend me a hand.

t_ch: 曾有贤者说，「想要别人善待你，你得先善待别人。」这话我觉得很受用。
t_en: A sage once said: "If you want someone to be kind to you, you must first be kind to others." I think this is most appropriate here.

t_ch: 当然，总有人觉得自己不会陷入困境。只是…谁又能保证奥赫玛的城墙能永远挺立呢？
t_en: Of course, there are always people who think they'll never find themselves in hardship. Except... who can ensure that Okhema's city walls will stand tall forever?

t_ch: 现在你有何打算？
t_en: What're you going to do now?

t_ch: 按照圣城法律，他们将对我进行审判，结果大概率不会仁慈。
t_en: According to the holy city's laws, they will make me stand trial. There's a high chance they won't be lenient.

t_ch: 坚持正确的事也要付出代价，何况我只是为了满足自己的施救欲，并不能代表正确。我已做好了接受惩罚的准备。
t_en: The price must be paid for doing the right thing, even more so for an ambiguously moral act like mine conducted to sate my own savior complex. I am fully ready to accept its consequences.

t_ch: 在最后的时刻到来前，我唯一的愿望就是能再吃一个苹果。
t_en: Before the final moment arrives, my only wish is to eat another apple.

t_ch: 苹果？
t_en: Apple?

t_ch: 是的，苹果是我最爱的食物。「每天一苹果，医生远离我」。
t_en: Yes, apples are my favorite food. "An apple a day keeps the doctor away."

t_ch: 可惜咱们的交流没能再深一些，不然我一定会和你探讨苹果的美味，以及其他更多事情……
t_en: 'Tis a pity we didn't get to know each other better, otherwise I would surely discuss the wonderful flavor of apples with you, along with many other matters...

t_ch: （说起来，重渊的小女孩还真送了我一个苹果…要交给他吗？）
t_en: (That reminds me, that girl in the Abyss gave me an apple... Should I give it to him?)

t_ch: 给出苹果。
t_en: Give him the apple.

t_ch: 哦，你居然会随身携带苹果？这可是贤者的十大必备特质之一，你很上道嘛！
t_en: Oh, you actually have an apple on you? This is one of the ten essential idiosyncrasies among sages. You learn fast!

t_ch: 谢谢…如此一来，我也就没什么遗憾了。
t_en: Thank you... I will have no regrets now.

t_ch: 苏克提瑞斯美美地吃起了苹果。吃到一半时，门口的卫兵走了进来。
t_en: Soctires gleefully crunches down on the apple. When he's halfway through eating, the cell's guard walks in.

t_ch: 不给。
t_en: Nope.

t_ch: 你没有给出那个苹果，苏克提瑞斯苍白地笑笑，低下了头。似乎也没什么可聊了。
t_en: You don't give him the apple. Soctires smiles palely and lowers his head — there is nothing left to be said.

t_ch: 在一片沉默中，门口的卫兵走了进来。
t_en: The cell's guard enters, cutting through that thick silence.

t_ch: 探视时间结束了，异邦的英雄，请回吧。
t_en: Visiting time is over. Outlander hero, it is time to leave.

t_ch: 你们会释放他吗？
t_en: Will you release him?

t_ch: 你们会处罚他吗？
t_en: Will you punish him?

t_ch: 他会面临怎样的命运？
t_en: What fate is in store for him?

t_ch: 这不是我能决定的，要看公民法庭会给出怎样的意见。
t_en: It is not up to me to decide. The public court will handle him.

t_ch: 据苏克提瑞斯说，他闯入圣城仓库，是为了帮助一伙受伤的外邦人——我们已经派人去查证了。这可能会影响他的量刑。
t_en: According to Soctires, he trespassed on the holy city's warehouse, all to help a group of wounded outlanders. We have already dispatched people to investigate. This may affect his sentencing.

t_ch: 审判将在三天后的<color=#dbc291ff>刻法勒广场</color>举行。届时也请您一同出席，见证圣城律法的公正。
t_en: The trial will be held at the <color=#dbc291ff>Kephale Plaza</color> in three days. We also invite you to attend, to witness the justice of the holy city's law.

t_ch: 三天后，刻法勒广场
t_en: Three days later, Kephale Plaza

t_ch: 好了，各位公民，请肃静。
t_en: All right, fellow citizens, order.

t_ch: 我是来自黎明云崖的元老伊特罗，{RUBY_B#律法之泰坦}塔兰顿{RUBY_E#}的祭司。我谨代表元老院的意志，前来主持这场审判。
t_en: I am Elder Etro from Dawncloud, priest of {RUBY_B#Law Titan}Talanton{RUBY_E#}. I am presiding over this trial representing the will of the Council of Elders.

t_ch: 今天我们聚在这里，是因为受人尊敬的公共讲师，苏克提瑞斯，闯进了圣城仓库行窃。
t_en: The reason we are gathered here today is that the dearly respected public speaker, Soctires, trespassed on the holy city's warehouse and committed theft.

t_ch: 按律法所言，偷窃应处以禁闭之刑。但考虑到作案动机，本案又不能简单依照先例判罚。
t_en: According to law, theft is a crime punishable by imprisonment. But in light of the motive of this case, it cannot be so easily judged based on precedent.

t_ch: 因此，今天我们遵循传统、召开公民法庭，由在场的诸位代表作出裁决。愿塔兰顿的公正与我们同在。
t_en: And so, as per the tradition that we uphold, we have opened up the public court, for everyone present to be the judge. May Talanton's justice be with us.

t_ch: 公民们纷纷为这番开场词鼓起掌来，直到元老示意掌声止息。
t_en: The citizens' applause rise in a crescendo in response to this opening, till the Elder gestures for the clapping to cease.

t_ch: 本案的争议在于，苏克提瑞斯之所以铤而走险，是为了救助一伙受伤的外邦人。然而，这是否能成为他减刑乃至免罪的理由？
t_en: The controversy in this case lies in the fact that Soctires risked his own well-being to help a group of wounded outlanders. However, is this enough reason for his sentence to be reduced, or even acquitted?

t_ch: 站在我右手边的是控方代表，仓库的主人，富人拉莱德。他认为应当重判，以免再有物资流出城外。
t_en: To my right is the prosecution's representative and owner of the warehouse, the wealthy Liluder. He believes that the punishment should be harsh, to prevent future supplies from leaving the city.

t_ch: 站在我左手边的是辩方代表，嫌犯的弟子，学者帕拉滕。他认为考虑到嫌犯确实救了许多人命，应当轻判。
t_en: To my left is the defendant's representative and student of the accused, Scholar Palaten. He believes that the sentence should be light, considering the actions of the accused did indeed save many lives.

t_ch: 双方均已提交了书面文件。在辩论开始之前，现场有哪位公民要提问吗？
t_en: Both parties have submitted their documents. Before the trial starts, do any citizens present have any questions?

t_ch: 作为罪人，我申请先对诸位公民说几句话。
t_en: As the sinner, I request to say a few words to everyone present.

t_ch: 哎呀，你这老头，都被抓了还打算妖言惑众？
t_en: Hey, old fart, you were caught red-handed, and you're still trying to charm your way out of this?

t_ch: 拉莱德，苏克提瑞斯有发言的权利。
t_en: Liluder, Soctires has the right to speak.

t_ch: 感谢伊特罗大人……
t_en: Thank you, Elder Etro...

t_ch: 苏克提瑞斯清了清嗓子，他的眼神仿佛一头被困在泥潭里的大地兽——这种生物临死前的反应不是暴怒，而是眼眶湿润。
t_en: Soctires clears his throat, his eyes mirroring a dromas ensnared in swampy mud — when such a creature is on the verge of death, its eyes are not one of wrath, but wet with tears.

t_ch: 在遥远的黄金年代，我们曾经以城邦为界、彼此分立；在并不遥远的纷争年代，我们又以城邦为由、彼此交战…这段历史大家应该很熟悉。
t_en: In the golden age long past, we were once divided as city-states, separate from one another. In the warring age not-so-long past, we once again divided ourselves as city-states, which became the reason for our battles... I'm sure everyone is very familiar with this stretch of history.

t_ch: 然而黑潮不会分辨城邦。在它眼里，我们都只是人，是弱小的存在。如今世上已经没有其他城邦了，那些我们熟知的名字早就消散在了风中。
t_en: But the black tide does not discriminate between cities. In its eyes, we are all humans, insignificant existences. In the present day, there are no other cities left, their names that which we are oh-so familiar scattered to the winds.

t_ch: 所以，外邦人其实并不存在——外邦是哪个邦？摆在我们面前的，只有要不要帮助一群需要帮助的人而已。
t_en: Which is why, in reality, outlanders don't exist — which city do they hail from? The question posed before us is merely about whether we want to help a group of people who need it.

t_ch: 至于我的罪名，完全不值得多费口舌。我可以接受惩罚，只希望各位能抓住这个机会、把当下的问题聊清楚——
t_en: As for my crime, precious time need not be spent on it. I can accept the punishment, but I only hope that everyone will take this chance to discuss the current question with clarity...

t_ch: 圣城对弱者到底该是何态度？我们究竟要把这座永恒之城引向何方？以上，再次感谢伊特罗大人。
t_en: What stance exactly does the holy city take with the underprivileged? Where exactly are we leading this Eternal City? That's all. I thank Elder Etro again for this chance.

t_ch: 那么，在辩论开始前，现场有哪位公民要提问吗？
t_en: Now then, before the trial starts, do any citizens present have any questions?

t_ch: 其实我很好奇，灰发的贵客对此会有何见解？作为和黄金裔并肩作战过的英雄，你的想法至关重要。
t_en: I am curious: How does our gray-haired guest view this situation? As the hero who fought alongside the Chrysos Heirs, your thoughts may be of crucial import.

t_ch: （在大地兽工坊的那场骚乱中，我选择了<color=#dbc291ff>为那个外邦人撑腰</color>，因此受到了苏克提瑞斯的赏识……）
t_en: (During the turmoil that occurred in the dromas workshop, I chose to <color=#dbc291ff>support the outlander</color> and thus gained Soctires' appreciation...)

t_ch: （在大地兽工坊的那场骚乱中，我选择了<color=#dbc291ff>声讨那个外邦人</color>，但他还是被苏克提瑞斯解了围。）
t_en: (During the turmoil that occurred in the dromas workshop, I chose to <color=#dbc291ff>go against the outlander</color>, but Soctires still managed to sort everything out for him.)

t_ch: （在大地兽工坊的那场骚乱中，我<color=#dbc291ff>没有支持任何一方</color>，只是静看事态发展。）
t_en: (During the turmoil that occurred in the dromas workshop, I <color=#dbc291ff>didn't support anyone</color> and just quietly watched things unfold.)

t_ch: （后来苏克提瑞斯邀请我详谈此事。在云石天宫的浴池里，我表达了<color=#dbc291ff>对外邦人的同情</color>。）
t_en: (Later, Soctires invited me to discuss this. I professed my <color=#dbc291ff>sympathies toward the outlanders</color> at the bath in the Marmoreal Palace.)

t_ch: （后来苏克提瑞斯邀请我详谈此事。在云石天宫的浴池里，我表达了<color=#dbc291ff>对外邦人的反感</color>。）
t_en: (Later, Soctires invited me to discuss this. I professed my <color=#dbc291ff>repulsion toward the outlanders</color> at the bath in the Marmoreal Palace.)

t_ch: （后来苏克提瑞斯邀请我详谈此事。在云石天宫的浴池里，我表达了<color=#dbc291ff>中立且客观的态度</color>。）
t_en: (Later, Soctires invited me to discuss this. I professed my <color=#dbc291ff>impartiality</color> at the bath in the Marmoreal Palace.)

t_ch: （再后来，我们在重渊遇见了那些需要帮助的外邦人。我为他们购买了<color=#dbc291ff>劣质的食物</color>。）
t_en: (After that, we once again met in the abyss the outsiders who needed aid in the abyss. There, I bought the outlanders some <color=#dbc291ff>low-quality meals</color>.)

t_ch: （再后来，我们在重渊遇见了那些需要帮助的外邦人。我为他们购买了<color=#dbc291ff>物美价廉的食物</color>。）
t_en: (After that, we once again met in the abyss the outsiders who needed aid. There, I bought the outlanders some <color=#dbc291ff>meals that were a good bargain</color>.)

t_ch: （再后来，我们在重渊遇见了那些需要帮助的外邦人。我为他们购买了<color=#dbc291ff>非常丰盛的食物</color>。）
t_en: (After that, we once again met in the abyss the outsiders who needed aid. There, I bought the outlanders some <color=#dbc291ff>luxurious meals</color>.)

t_ch: 这么看，我一直以来的观点还是挺明显的……
t_en: From what I did, I think my standpoint is pretty obvious...

t_ch: 你抬起头，苏克提瑞斯露出释然的眼神，仿佛在说：「小友，遵循你内心的态度就好」。
t_en: You raise your head, meeting Soctires' gaze of respite, as if saying: "Young friend, stay true to your heart."

t_ch: 支持学者帕拉滕的辩护。
t_en: Support Scholar Palaten's defense.

t_ch: 我支持学者帕拉滕的观点。为救人性命而犯下的错误，不应完全按照罪行看待。
t_en: I support Scholar Palaten's point. Mistakes incurred from saving lives should not be solely judged as a crime.

t_ch: 我曾亲眼见过那些外邦人，他们确实处在危机之中。如果不是苏克提瑞斯为他们搞到了药，后果不堪设想。
t_en: I once saw those outlanders with my own eyes, and they were indeed in dire danger. If not for Soctires securing medicine for them, the consequences would have been unimaginable.

t_ch: 出于这个理由，我认为……
t_en: In light of this reason, I believe that...

t_ch: 应该减刑。
t_en: The sentencing should be reduced.

t_ch: 应该缓刑。
t_en: The sentencing should be suspended.

t_ch: 应该无罪！
t_en: He is not guilty!

t_ch: 啥？这么严重的事情，无罪？！还有王法吗？还有法律吗？
t_en: What? Not guilty of such a heinous crime!? Does the law even exist in your eyes?

t_ch: 好家伙，吾师果然没看错你。
t_en: Hah, Master wasn't wrong about you at all.

t_ch: 支持富人拉莱德的控诉。
t_en: Support Wealthy Liluder's complaint.

t_ch: 我认为拉莱德说得有道理。无论是否出于善意，人总要为他做过的事负责。
t_en: I think Liluder speaks true. Regardless of motive, he has to be responsible for his actions.

t_ch: 这个世界上不缺少因善而生的恶。倘若动机能替行为辩护，那谁都会说自己「本意是好的」。
t_en: This world has no lack of evil borne of compassion. If motives could replace a defense, everyone would say their "intentions are good."

t_ch: 出于这个理由，我认为……
t_en: In light of this reason, I believe that...

t_ch: 应该依法量刑。
t_en: The sentencing should be imposed according to law.

t_ch: 应该严判重判。
t_en: The sentencing should be imposed more harshly.

t_ch: 应该直接处死！
t_en: Execution is in order!

t_ch: 这…偷盗的最高量刑也不过是监禁而已啊！
t_en: Wha... The harshest punishment for theft is at best imprisonment!

t_ch: {F#好丫头}{M#好小子}，我就知道你是深明大义之人。
t_en: {F#Lass}{M#Lad}, I knew you were a righteous character.

t_ch: 保持中立。
t_en: Maintain neutrality.

t_ch: （如果我选择中立，就没法在二人接下来的辩论中插嘴。我真要放弃站台发言的机会吗？）
t_en: (If I choose to stay neutral, I won't be able to jump into their debate. Do I really want to abandon a chance to speak at the podium?)

t_ch: 或许支持某一方更好…
t_en: It might be better to support one or the other...

t_ch: 还是别掺和这事了…
t_en: Better not get involved in this matter...

t_ch: 我要保留自己的态度，这场审判我不会支持任何一方。
t_en: I will maintain my own stance and not support any side in this trial.

t_ch: 那么，双方代表可以开始辩论了。
t_en: Now then, both parties can begin their debate.

t_ch: 元老话音刚落，两位代表就像竞技场上的角斗士般对峙起来，吵得唾沫横飞。直到双方都精疲力尽、口干舌燥，局面才有所缓和……
t_en: As soon as the Elder ends his last word, both representatives face off like gladiators in an arena, their verbosity locked in fury and spit. It isn't until both sides have exhausted their energy and spittle, that the situation finally begins to calm down...

t_ch: 真是一场激烈的辩论。接下来，请大家投票表决……
t_en: What an intense clash of words. Next, will everyone please vote on their decision...

t_ch: 在那之前，介意我补充几句吗？
t_en: Before that, may I add a few words?

t_ch: …小灰毛，我原以为你身为外邦英雄，在诸位公民面前必有高论——没想到，竟然如此胡搅蛮缠！
t_en: ...Gray Hair, you're supposed to be an outlander hero who must have something grandiose to say before the people. I never figured you for a jabbering troublemaker!

t_ch: 拉莱德代表，如果你有异议，还请堂堂正正地驳倒{F#她}{M#他}。
t_en: Representative Liluder, if you have any objections, please respond to {F#her}{M#him} without contempt.

t_ch: 哼，众所周知，偷盗犯法，何况是把管控品倒卖给外邦人——这怎么能算善举呢？明明是罪加一等才对。
t_en: Hmph, as everyone knows, theft is a crime, let alone selling regulated goods to outlanders. How can this be considered a benevolent act? There should be another charge slapped on top of his crime.

t_ch: 要我说，就应该直接处死苏克提瑞斯，这样才能威慑潜在的犯罪分子。
t_en: If it were up to me, I would have Soctires executed. That'll set an example for other potential criminals.

t_ch: 拉莱德，不要公报私仇！大家都知道你对吾师怀恨在心，只因他拆穿了你诬陷外邦人的伎俩！
t_en: Liluder, this is not a place to pursue personal vengeance! Everyone knows you've always hated Master, all because he thwarted your petty attempt to frame the outlanders!

t_ch: 我这是在为奥赫玛的利益着想。否则还不等下个门扉时到来，圣城仓库就要被搬空了。
t_en: I only have Okhema's interests at heart. Otherwise, by the next Entry Hour, the holy city's warehouse would have been plundered clean!

t_ch: （现场有这么多人…或许我应该试着调动这些公民的情绪。）
t_en: (So many people present... Maybe I should try to appeal to the public's emotions.)
